concept: Affirm / To Confirm the Wholeness of
description: The act of stating with confidence that something is true or valid; to support and uphold. It is an act of strengthening and confirming the reality and worth of something.
rationale: Composed from the established roots 'ka' (to stand, to be present with strength) and 'o' (wholeness). The word's structure literally means 'to make whole with strength' or 'to stand for the whole.' This defines affirmation as a powerful act of support that confirms the integrity and completeness of a person or idea.
